program_name: Environmental Sciences for the Ecological Transition

academic_requirements.summary: Applicants need a minimum three-year undergraduate degree or equivalent and must show a suitable scientific background for environmental systems and ecological transition studies.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: Bachelor diploma or equivalent; a minimum three-year undergraduate degree is required.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: Applicants must have basic knowledge in Mathematics, Physics, Chemistry, Biology, Environmental Sciences, and Ecology, including agricultural and human systems.
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: [uncertain] Country-based specific entry-title and GPA requirements apply through the University of Padua entry-title requirements page. The 2026/27 educational-offer page also says eligible candidates are assessed on a minimum degree grade, but it does not publish the exact threshold reviewed here.

accepted_proofs: The programme's 2026/27 educational-offer page lists the following B2-equivalent thresholds: TOEFL iBT 80, IELTS 6.0, Cambridge ESOL 173, Trinity College London ISE II, Oxford Test of English 126, Gatehouse GA 1, Pearson PTE General 3, or Pearson PTE Academic 65.
A provisional statement of results issued by the certifying body while waiting for the final certificate.
A previous degree title delivered entirely in English, documented through the procedures stated on the University language-admission page.
exemption_rules: Applicants are exempt if they hold a degree title delivered entirely in English and the documentation explicitly states the language of instruction.
Applicants are exempt if they are native English speakers from one of the citizenships listed by the University of Padua on the 2026/27 English language admission page.
language_requirements.deadline: [uncertain] The 2026/27 pages reviewed state the B2 requirement and accepted proof methods, but they do not separately publish a programme-specific submission deadline for this programme on the pages reviewed.

application_deadline_eu.notes: The third call is only for EU citizens and non-EU citizens residing in Italy.

application_deadline_non_eu.notes: The third call is not available to non-EU citizens residing outside Italy.

required_documents.document_name: Valid passport or valid ID card
required_documents.notes: EU or equivalent applicants can use a valid ID card; other applicants normally upload a passport.
Required only for non-EU applicants already residing in Italy.
Documents must be official and written or translated in Italian or English.

required_documents.document_name: Bachelor's degree certificate or academic qualification
required_documents.notes: Upload if already obtained; no legalised documents are required at application stage.
Listed in the official general document checklist for applications through apply.unipd.it.
Listed in the official general document checklist for applications through apply.unipd.it.

required_documents.document_name: English language proficiency certificate or other accepted proof
required_documents.notes: Required unless the applicant qualifies for one of the University of Padua exemption routes.

required_documents.document_name: Official translation of academic documents into Italian or English
required_documents.notes: Required if the original academic documents are issued in another language.

required_documents.document_name: Bachelor's degree translated into Italian or English and legalised
required_documents.notes: Apostille may replace legalisation where applicable.

required_documents.document_name: Transcript of Records translated into Italian or English and legalised
required_documents.notes: The transcript should state passed exams, grades, and preferably credits and syllabus information.
Required only for non-EU applicants residing outside Italy.

required_documents.document_name: Valid identity document or passport and residence permit/Assicurata
required_documents.notes: EU or equivalent applicants provide an ID document or passport; non-EU applicants also provide a residence permit or residence-permit application receipt where applicable.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: No standard written entry exam or admission test is stated on the 2026/27 programme factsheet.
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: Admission is based on meeting the curricular and language requirements. The 2026/27 educational-offer page says eligible candidates are assessed on minimum degree grade and B2 English proficiency.

source_quotes.quote: A minimum three-year undergraduate degree (or equivalent) is required

uncertainty_notes: This programme is new for 2026/27 and the official pages are not fully harmonised: the central programme page shows 'Language Italian' and only 'Legnaro', while the official apply.unipd.it factsheet and the official educational-offer page describe the programme as English-taught and held in Legnaro and Padua.
The 2026/27 educational-offer page states that eligible candidates are assessed on a minimum degree grade, but the exact threshold was not found on the official pages reviewed.
The programme pages reviewed state the B2 English requirement and accepted proof routes, but they do not clearly publish a separate language-certificate submission deadline for this programme.
No separate 2026/27 PDF admission notice was found; the official apply.unipd.it programme factsheet was used as the programme-specific admissions source.
